name: Syd's Turkey Gravy
recipeInstructions:
Assuming you are going to roast a turkey in the oven, it will be in a roasting pan of some kind. I am also assuming this turkey had giblets (you know, liver, heart, gizzard and neck). While the turkey is roasting, put the giblets in a large pot with plenty of water and salt. Bring them to a boil and let simmer until done.
After you remove the turkey from the roaster, there should be lots of juices that have accumulated during roasting. Pour these juices into another container and skim the fat off (or use a fat skimming cup or brush). Then put the de-fatted drippings back into the pan. Add some of the liquid from cooking the giblets if needed to make 2 cups. Whisk flour into it, and let simmer until thickened.
Scrape up all of the "goodies" from the bottom of the pan to make the gravy extra-flavorful. If it gets too thick, add more liquid. If it isn't thick enough, add more flour and continue to whisk to keep out most of the lumps. You can chop up the giblets finely and add them to the gravy as it is almost done - just to heat through.
If you have to adjust the color, a dash of Kitchen Bouquet will do the trick - not much.
